business.headline: Lupin, Aurobindo, Sun Pharma fall up to 4%: Why are pharma stocks falling today?Dalal Street fell sharply after Donald Trump announced a phased tariff plan for imported generic medicines. The proposal has raised concerns over costs, manufacturing shifts and the long-term outlook for Indian drugmakers.

Sensex tanks over 500 points: 3 reasons why the stock market is falling todayThe BSE Sensex was trading at 76,866.60, down 603.51 points or 0.78%, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 160.75 points, or 0.66%, to 24,026.95 around 9:35 am.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Brent crude climbs above $92The BSE Sensex fell 367.92 points, or 0.47%, to 77,102.19, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 99.10 points, or 0.41%, to 24,088.60 at around 9:20 am.

Stock market today: Will Sensex, Nifty fall as crude crosses $92 mark?GIFT Nifty futures were trading around 24,117 at 7:56 am IST, indicating that the Nifty50 could open below Tuesday's close of 24,187.70.

Are you eligible for additional UPS benefits? Here's what govt saysReplying to a question in the Lok Sabha, Minister of State for Finance Pankaj Chaudhary said that 25,756 retired Central government subscribers are eligible to claim additional benefits under the UPS.

Volkswagen weighs JSW partnership for India businessVolkswagen is in advanced talks with JSW Group over an investment in Skoda Auto Volkswagen India. The proposed deal could reshape the carmaker's India strategy as competition intensifies.

Everyone wants AI engineers. But who will make sure AI actually works?As companies increasingly deploy AI across banking, healthcare, customer service and other sectors, another talent shortage may be quietly emerging; professionals who can test whether these systems are accurate, secure and reliable before they reach users.

Maruti Suzuki announces fresh price hike: Check how much more you'll have to payMaruti Suzuki will raise vehicle prices by up to Rs 30,000 from August. The move marks its second portfolio-wide increase in about two months amid sustained cost pressures.

Can ITR portal handle the filing rush before July 31? Here's what government saysIn a written reply in Parliament, the government said that while some users may experience occasional delays during periods of heavy traffic, the portal has remained largely stable despite a significant increase in the number of taxpayers using it this year.

RBI's foreign currency deposit scheme for NRIs explainedThe RBI has rolled out a concessional swap facility to revive FCNR(B) deposits after a sharp fall in inflows. The move could help banks raise rates, but NRIs still need to weigh currency needs and taxes.

Dalal Street ends lower on HDFC Bank drag; midcaps continue to outperformThe BSE Sensex closed 238.41 points, or 0.31%, lower at 77,470.11, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 50.80 points, or 0.21%, to settle at 24,187.70.

Gift Deed vs Sale Deed vs Will: Here's how to pick the right way to transfer propertyA single document can decide how smoothly your property changes hands and whether your family faces disputes later. So, should you opt for a Gift Deed, Sale Deed or a Will?

India's biggest private banks are quietly reducing their workforce. Why?HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank and Axis Bank ended FY26 with more than 13,000 fewer employees. The decline reflects attrition, redeployment and technology-led productivity rather than mass layoffs.

Who does Warren Buffett credit for his extraordinary success?Buffett bought his first shares at the age of 11, purchasing three shares of Cities Service for $38 each. That small investment marked the beginning of a career that would eventually turn him into one of the world's most successful investors.

SBI Funds Management lists at nearly 7% premium: Should you buy, sell or hold?SBI Funds Management listed at a modest premium after its heavily subscribed IPO. The debut beat the issue price but fell short of grey market expectations, leaving investors split on booking gains or holding.

Why are farmers marching to Delhi? Key reasons behind the protestWhile farmer unions have paused their next move after Haryana assured talks with the Centre and the release of detained leaders, they remain firm in opposing the proposed India-US trade deal.
Sensex, Nifty open lower as HDFC Bank drags; IT stocks cap lossesThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 54.36 points to 77,654.16, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 8.70 points to 24,229.80 as of 9:25 am.

SBI Funds Management IPO to list today: Will it open strongly? Check GMPSBI Funds Management will make its market debut on Tuesday after strong IPO demand. Grey market trends and institutional buying have kept expectations of healthy listing gains alive.

Will India raise ethanol blending beyond 20%? Here's what the Minister saysResponding to concerns about vehicle performance, the Minister said the government has not received any widespread or verified complaints from vehicle manufacturers, automobile associations or consumer groups regarding engine failure, fuel pump problems, corrosion or water contamination linked to E20 fuel.

Dalal Street ends lower on weak bank earnings, rising Middle East tensionsThe BSE Sensex fell 442.93 points, or 0.57%, to close at 77,708.52. The NSE Nifty50 also ended in the red, slipping 95.80 points, or 0.39%, to settle at 24,238.50.

entertainment.headline: Growth is fair education: Rajkummar Rao on CJP's students protest, police violence Rajkummar Rao backed the student protest at Jantar Mantar and urged peace and dialogue. His post adds to growing public support as students press for education reforms and accountability.

Lock Upp 2: Dheeraj Dhoopar's wife Vinny Arora backs him after chit controversy Actor Dheeraj Dhoopar's family meeting on Lock Upp 2 came under scrutiny after chits were allegedly sent inside. His actor-wife Vinny Arora called it an emotional mistake, while viewers questioned the show's lenient response.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan mints Rs 20 crore in advance bookings, 7 lakh tickets sold Jana Nayagan has crossed Rs 20 crore in domestic advance bookings, including blocked seats, ahead of release. The strong pre-sales have kept trade hopeful despite delays, an HD leak and the film trailing Vijay's last two openers.

Chauhaan: Zeeshan Ayyub distances himself, says his voice was misused in teaser Actor Mohammed Zeeshan Ayyub said he is not part of Chauhaan and had dubbed only one line without any reference. His clarification came after the teaser drew objections and his voice was replaced following his protest.

Police trapped us and started hitting us: TV actor Sehban Azim on CJP protest Television actor Sehban Azim said he was injured during a police lathicharge at a CJP protest in Delhi. He claimed students and women were beaten as demonstrators were stopped before reaching Jantar Mantar.

Made for people's hearts, not the box office: Pankaj Tripathi backs Ohh My Dog Pankaj Tripathi has opened up about his decision to support Amit Rai's Ohh My Dog, saying the film was made to touch hearts rather than chase box-office numbers. The actor stressed that meaningful stories and social impact matter more than the size of a role or commercial success.

Why was Vijay silent during Jana Nayagan leak? Director H Vinoth reveals Director H Vinoth explained the reasonwhy Vijay avoided commenting on the Jana Nayagan leak during promotions in Chennai. He said a public appeal could have triggered political criticism while the police handled the case.

Why Aryan Khan never smiles for cameras? Raghav Juyal finally explains Raghav Juyal opened up about the reason behind Shah Rukh Khan's son Aryan Khan keeping a straight face during his appearances in public. The actor, who worked with Aryan on The Ba**ds of Bollywood, shared his thoughts in a recent interview.

Preity Zinta warns against 'hijacking' of CJP's students protest, calls for dialogue Preity Zinta has reacted to the ongoing CJP student protest, supporting the demand for educational reforms while warning against attempts to "hijack" peaceful demonstrations. The actor called for meaningful dialogue between all stakeholders to protect the future of the youth.

Tovino Thomas, Revathy, Pa Ranjith condemn police action on CJP student protesters Actors Tovino Thomas, Revathy Asha and director Pa Ranjith condemned the police action on CJP student protesters at Jantar Mantar in Delhi. Their statements linked the protests over NEET irregularities to wider demands for accountability and education reform.

I am fine: Amitabh Bachchan says viral post about surgery was misunderstood Amitabh Bachchan said his blog post on surgery and intensive care was not about his own health but Argentina's World Cup final loss. His clarification came after fans read the note as a personal health update and raised concerns.

Naseeruddin Shah slams CJP protest crackdown, compares goons with ICE agents Naseeruddin Shah has reacted to the violence during CJP's Chalo Sansad march, condemning the treatment of protesters and comparing the attackers with American ICE agents. The actor also urged the youth to stay hopeful and continue their fight.

Spider-Man Brand New Day final trailer: Peter Parker prepares for epic Hulk showdown The makers of Spider-Man: Brand New Day have released a new trailer that revisits Peter Parker's emotional journey after No Way Home. It also teases an intense showdown between Spider-Man and a furious Hulk ahead of the film's release.

IMAX projectionist shares stunning theatre view, says don't miss The Odyssey in 70mm An IMAX projectionist has shared a stunning view from inside the projection booth during a screening of Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. Calling the experience extraordinary, he urged audiences not to miss the film in IMAX 70mm if they get the chance.

Shabana Azmi suffers asthma attack during CJP protest, says tear gas triggered it Shabana Azmi suffered an asthma attack after tear gas was used at her during the CJP protest in Delhi. She said she returned after recovering, backed the students during the march.

Godzilla x Kong actor Kaylee Hottle dies in car accident at 18 Kaylee Hottle, who played Jia in the Godzilla films, has died at 18 after a car accident in Maryland. Her father confirmed the news on Facebook.

If she's with me, my instinct is to protect her: Raghav on shielding Shehnaz Gill Raghav Juyal addressed the viral birthday clip showing him shielding Shehnaaz Gill through a crowd outside the venue. He said his reaction was instinctive, rooted in his upbringing, and not about appearances.

Exclusive: Matt Damon recalls the risk that changed his and Ben Affleck's lives Matt Damon and Ben Affleck's four-decade friendship has remained a constant through their personal and professional highs and lows. The duo also risked losing Good Will Hunting rather than compromise on their creative vision, a gamble that eventually led to Oscar success.

I don't get bothered: Karanvir Bohra reacts to divorce rumours with wife Teejay Actor Karanvir Bohra has responded to rumours about his marriage with Teejay Sidhu. The two have been married for a decade now.

general news.headline: Don't have time to watch videos: CJI on plea against crackdown on CJP protestersChief Justice Surya Kant declined to urgently hear a petition over "police brutality" against students protesting at Jantar Mantar. "Don't waste our time," the CJI told the lawyer who mentioned the matter.

AAP targets Rahul Gandhi over Congress protest, comes under fireAAP's attacks on the Congress over Rahul Gandhi's protest triggered a backlash from Opposition leaders and social media users, who accused the party of undermining the ongoing anti-paper leak agitation. The BJP also joined the row, turning the spat into a broader political slugfest.

25 days of fast, 10 minutes with Government: Inside Wangchuk's night at MedantaSonam Wangchuk was shifted from Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta after winning a High Court order allowing treatment at a hospital of his choice during the 25th day of his hunger strike.

How a bloodied Rahul Gandhi gave Congress the image it wantedRahul Gandhi and senior Congress leaders were detained outside the Prime Minister's residence during a flash protest on Tuesday. It helped Congress reclaim the space over the paper leak issue while escalating pressure on the Modi government.

Sonam Wangchuk, Cockroach Janta Party and the politics of paranoiaIn the span of a year, Sonam Wangchuk has gone from being branded a Chinese sympathiser to being accused of serving the Indian establishment. Such contradictions expose the absurdity of political paranoia.

Surat Police bust AI-powered cyber fraud network run by 18-year-old dropoutDespite dropping out after Class 11, police said Rohit Virendrasinh Shakya became an expert coder by the age of 16 and used AI tools and Telegram channels to develop fake applications that closely resembled genuine apps. He operated on a subscription model, selling the malicious APK files to cybercriminals for Rs 15,000 a month.

Monsoon clouds swallow 95% of India, Isro satellite images show: What to expectIsro's INSAT satellite images taken in the early hours of July 22 show cloud covering nearly the whole of India, with thunderstorm tops climbing 16 km. Here is what cloud top temperature, the monsoon trough and IMD's latest bulletin actually tell us about the rain ahead.

Even the well-heeled Gen Z joined the protest at Jantar Mantar. I found out whyWell-heeled teenagers who could have stayed home have chosen to stand in the rain, breathe tear gas and risk a lathicharge while marching from Jantar Mantar to Parliament. That, perhaps, is a break in the pattern and reveals something might have shifted.

Did FIFA let Argentina's Leandro Paredes off the hook after World Cup final brawl?Leandro Paredes' red card after the brawl following the World Cup final has been deleted by FIFA. Paredes was seen coming to blows with Eric Garcia and Gavi after the end of the game.

Live: 16 Delhi Metro stations including Rajiv Chowk, ITO shut amid protestsCJP Protest in Delhi Live Updates: Protesters of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) reclaimed Delhi's Jantar Mantar on Tuesday morning after police dismantled the protest stage and tents overnight following a day of clashes between demonstrators and security personnel. Heavy deployment of Delhi Police and Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel remained in place around the protest site. Leader of the Opposition Rahul Gandhi, whose absence from the protest had drawn attention as several opposition leaders and prominent citizens thronged the site, later led a group of Congress MPs in a sit-in outside the Prime Minister's residence. This led to dramatic scenes as police removed Gandhi and other lawmakers from the area. Gandhi was briefly detained before being released. Tensions escalated again late Tuesday when stone-pelting was reported on Delhi Police and RAF personnel in Connaught Place, a short distance from Jantar Mantar. Stay with Indiatoday.in for latest updates

Shashi Tharoor shares Amitabh Bachchan's 2012 police action tweet amid CJP protestThe Congress MP revived Amitabh Bachchan's 2012 tweet criticising police action against protesters in Delhi as the row over the crackdown on NEET protesters deepened.

Pakistan seeks $10 billion US currency backstop after Iran war diplomacyPakistan has sought a USD 10 billion exchange stabilisation facility from the United States. The request could ease pressure on reserves and the rupee while signalling closer ties with Washington.

IMAX projectionist shares stunning theatre view, says don't miss The Odyssey in 70mmAn IMAX projectionist has shared a stunning view from inside the projection booth during a screening of Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. Calling the experience extraordinary, he urged audiences not to miss the film in IMAX 70mm if they get the chance.

Why cops wanted to make AI chatbot an accomplice in Bengaluru triple murderA triple murder in Bengaluru has become more than just another crime story. Bengaluru Police allege that the accused spent six months consulting Google's Gemini chatbot to plan the killings, making it one of their first murder probes in which AI is suspected to have played such an extensive role.

Sensex tanks over 500 points: 3 reasons why the stock market is falling todayThe BSE Sensex was trading at 76,866.60, down 603.51 points or 0.78%, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 160.75 points, or 0.66%, to 24,026.95 around 9:35 am.

Is DK Shivakumar beating EC's SIR in Karnataka?The DK Shivakumar-led Congress government in Karnataka has launched a door-to-door drive to issue Permanent Residence Certificates and caste certificates. The BJP is alleging that the move is aimed at protecting the Congress's "illegal vote bank" from the SIR exercise being conducted by the Election Commission.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan mints Rs 20 crore in advance bookings, 7 lakh tickets soldJana Nayagan has crossed Rs 20 crore in domestic advance bookings, including blocked seats, ahead of release. The strong pre-sales have kept trade hopeful despite delays, an HD leak and the film trailing Vijay's last two openers.

CJP appeals to supporters to stop sending food to protest sites as supplies pile upFood deliveries poured into Delhi's Jantar Mantar from all over the country until Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) organisers urged supporters to stop ordering more.

I am fine: Amitabh Bachchan says viral post about surgery was misunderstoodAmitabh Bachchan said his blog post on surgery and intensive care was not about his own health but Argentina's World Cup final loss. His clarification came after fans read the note as a personal health update and raised concerns.

Think India will bludgeon past Zimbabwe in the T20I series? Think againZIM vs IND: India may be the more experienced side heading into the T20I series, but Zimbabwe can be a dangerous proposition at home and should not be underestimated.

Two engineers want to touch Saturn's rings. Nasa thinks their plan could workNasa-backed concepts aim to sample Saturn's rings and send tiny probes through them. The proposals could answer long-standing questions about the rings' origin, age and structure.

TVK calls for scrapping NEET, seeks full state control over medical educationTVK has demanded that NEET be scrapped and states get full control over medical education. The stand sharpens Tamil Nadu's political battle over education and federal powers.

Trump announces up to 200% tariffs on generic drugs, move may hit IndiaAnnouncing the decision on his Truth Social platform, Trump said the policy is designed to encourage pharmaceutical companies to establish manufacturing facilities in the US.

Rahul Gandhi wanted political headlines, not solutions for students: PradhanDharmendra Pradhan's remarks came hours after Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi, staged a protest outside the Prime Minister's residence over the police action against students protesting the NEET paper leak.

sports.headline: David Warner pleads guilty to drink driving, faces up to 9 months in jail David Warner pleaded guilty to a mid-range drink-driving charge in Sydney through his lawyer. The conviction could bring jail time and trigger a review of his Sydney Thunder captaincy.

Rohit Sharma proves Rohit Sharma isn't India's ODI problem Rohit Sharma answered the only question worth asking of him at Lord's. What India actually need answered are the harder ones: a bowling attack built around one man's fitness, a potential lead spinner being overlooked, and search for all-rounders without a plan.

Did FIFA let Argentina's Leandro Paredes off the hook after World Cup final brawl? Leandro Paredes' red card after the brawl following the World Cup final has been deleted by FIFA. Paredes was seen coming to blows with Eric Garcia and Gavi after the end of the game.

Chelsea take sly dig at Arsenal after completing record Morgan Rogers deal Chelsea completed a record British deal to sign Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa after beating Arsenal to him. The Blues decided to a cheeky dig at their London rivals after Rogers picked them over the current Premier League champions.

Think India will bludgeon past Zimbabwe in the T20I series? Think again ZIM vs IND: India may be the more experienced side heading into the T20I series, but Zimbabwe can be a dangerous proposition at home and should not be underestimated.

Tilak Varma gives perfect response to strike-rate critics. Here's what he said Tilak Varma pushed back against questions over his reduced strike-rate before the Zimbabwe series. Tilak has struggled to maintain a consistent strike-rate over the last couple of years in T20Is.

Fresh off Lord's heroics, Rohit Sharma arrives in style for The Hundred opener Rohit Sharma turned up at the Oval for The Hundred opener after his fighting 138 at Lord's and was seen with fans before an intense chat with Nita Ambani. The visit for the match came on the heels of his hundred against England, which effectively shut down rumours of retirement.

La Liga chief accuses FIFA of destroying football, calls for Infantino to resign La Liga president Javier Tebas accused FIFA of "destroying the football industry" through the expansion of international competitions and said president Gianni Infantino should resign.

India boxing team hit by luggage chaos ahead of Commonwealth Games campaign India's boxing contingent suffered an early setback to its Commonwealth Games campaign on Tuesday after nearly half the team's luggage failed to arrive from Belfast.

Football's strangest red card? Player sent-off for urinating next to bench Brazilian forward Micael was shown a rare red card before kick-off in the Copa Paulista after urinating beside the substitutes' bench. However, the team did not have to play with a man short.

Chennai Grand Masters: D Gukesh suffers second loss, Sarin beats Erigaise World champion D Gukesh suffered his second consecutive defeat at the Chennai Grand Masters after losing to GM M Pranesh, while GM Nihal Sarin stunned Arjun Erigaisi in the penultimate round on Tuesday.

When in doubt, talk to Ashish Nehra: Ashok Sharma ahead of potential India debutIndia fast bowler Ashok Sharma credited Gujarat Titans head coach Ashish Nehra for his rapid rise, revealing the former India pacer was always available for advice as he prepares for a likely T20I debut against Zimbabwe.

Rohit Sharma's retirement speculation wasn't merely outside noise: AshwinFormer India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin believes there was substance to the speculation surrounding Rohit Sharma's ODI future. Speaking on his YouTube channel, Ashwin said that there is no smoke without fire.

Pressure is intense: Referee Anthony Taylor retires from football officiatingReferee Anthony Taylor has announced his retirement from football officiating with immediate effect, saying the intense pressure and constant scrutiny of the job played a key role in his decision.

Murali Sreeshankar eyes Glasgow gold after strong injury comebackIndia's premier long jumper Murali Sreeshankar believes his finest performances are still ahead of him as he prepares for the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, saying a "big jump" is around the corner after making an impressive return from a career-threatening injury.

India's recent T20I slump due to overplaying in Asia, says Tilak VarmaVice-captain Tilak Varma addressed India's recent T20I slump during the pre-series press conference in Harare. Varma argued that the team lost all six T20Is in the UK because they spent the majority of the last two years playing in Asia.

BCCI cracks down: Deliberate front-foot no-balls to invite match suspensionThe BCCI has revised its domestic playing conditions, introducing match-long suspensions for deliberate front-foot no-balls and non-landing deliveries. The changes also include revisions to end-of-day play, final-innings declarations and wicketkeeping rules ahead of the new domestic season.

India tour still on? Bangladesh hopeful despite Mustafizur Rahman IPL sagaBangladesh remains hopeful of hosting India in September and believes sports diplomacy can help ease tensions sparked by the Mustafizur Rahman controversy.

Vinicius Jr. gets face surgery, looks unrecognisable after FIFA World CupBrazil star Vinicius Jr. has reportedly undergone a chin harmonisation procedure after the FIFA World Cup. Images of his transformed appearance are going viral ahead of his return to Real Madrid.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is special, manage him well: Sikandar Raza to IndiaZIM vs IND: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza has urged India to carefully manage teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, backing the 15-year-old to become a generational talent if nurtured properly.

Prannoy, Devika knocked out as India make disappointing China Open startHS Prannoy and Devika Sihag suffered first-round exits at the China Open as India's campaign got off to a disappointing start. PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen and Ayush Shetty begin their campaigns on Wednesday.

India favourites to win T20I series vs Zimbabwe? Sikandar Raza does not think soZIM vs IND: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza has rejected the notion that India are overwhelming favourites for the upcoming T20I series, saying both teams have an equal chance of winning.

Hockey: India name World Cup squad with a good mix of youth and experienceHockey India have named a 20-member men's World Cup squad led by Harmanpreet Singh, with youth and experience across all lines. India have been drawn alongside Wales, England and Pakistan in the group stages of the tournament, starting August 15.

Want cheap Australian Open tickets? You may have to act faster in 2027The Australian Open will cap allocations of its popular A$49 ground passes during the opening week of the 2027 tournament after crowding and long queues marred this year's event in Melbourne.

Gambhir bashed for KL Rahul call: Like Samson, Sooryavanshi, you screwed him upKrishnamachari Srikkanth criticised Gautam Gambhir and the team management for sending KL Rahul in at No. 6 during India's chase at Lord's. His remarks have revived questions over Rahul's role after India lost the ODI series to England.

Falklands banner backlash: Do Argentina stars face a Premier League ban?Argentina players sparked a row after displaying a Falklands banner following their World Cup win over England. However, any move to revoke Premier League work visas would be highly unlikely.

Thank You, Lionel Messi, for giving the world one last bit of magicLionel Messi may have possibly signed off from the international football like a veteran magician leaving his stage for the final time. During the last two World Cup campaigns, Messi showed how he was able to galvanise the Argentina team for a common goal.

Who needs trophies? Loyal Argentina fans give hero's welcome to losing finalistsArgentina's beaten World Cup finalists arrived in Buenos Aires on Monday. Thousands greeted them with flares, drums and chants, underscoring loyalty that outlasted the missing trophy.

Pat Cummins returns as Australia pick full-strength squad for Bangladesh TestsAUS vs BAN: Pat Cummins, Josh Hazlewood and Nathan Lyon have returned to Australia's 13-man squad for the two-Test World Test Championship series against Bangladesh in Darwin and Mackay.

Inside Spain's World Cup party in Madrid: Footballers turn DJs for 2 million fansSpain's FIFA World Cup winners turned Madrid's Cibeles celebration into a giant DJ-led street party. The homecoming fused state ceremony, football folklore and viral fan moments before nearly two million supporters.

Have FIFA World Cup finals become boring this century?Spain's 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina brought the curtain down on another unforgettable World Cup. Yet after four weeks of drama, goals and upsets, the final once again struggled to match the spectacle that came before it. Has football's biggest match become the most boring?

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
